name: Force 3-CTA Residency On The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling

summary.name: Force 3-CTA Residency On The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling
summary.hypothesis: The current PTX anchor is fast but structurally pinned at 200 registers, occupancy_limit_registers=2, and about 16.62% active warps. The round-29 PTX launch-bounds probe showed that forcing 3-CTA residency can move the machine state materially, but it also exposed a PTX-specific barrier pathology. The non-PTX 128x128 sibling is the cleanest next branch to carry that occupancy experiment because it is already a correctness-proven alternate surface at 24.183295 ms and starts slightly leaner at 196 registers with nearly the same plateau signature. Restoring the exact sibling surface and then forcing its hot-band kernel toward 3 resident CTAs is therefore the best bounded aggressive test: it asks whether the occupancy win survives better on the non-PTX control/export path than it did on the PTX microkernel path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and latency hiding on the non-PTX 128x128 sibling surface.
summary.risk: Moderate. Correctness risk is low because the base sibling surface is already known-good, but performance risk remains because forcing a 3-CTA budget can still trade registers for spills or expose new synchronization costs.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime with a >0.15 ms improvement threshold over the current 24.168880 ms run
ranking_notes: This is the cleanest way to keep pushing the occupancy thesis without repeating the exact PTX-local failure mode from round 29.
The sibling path is close enough to the current anchor that the implementation can stay bounded to a restore plus launch-budget change.
The user guidance favors this kind of structural probe over another plateau replay.
summary.stop_condition: Stop this family if launch__registers_per_thread does not fall materially below the sibling baseline or if occupancy rises but barrier cost explodes into another round-29 style regression.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
